sections.instruction: Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: Many past owners made changes to
Sir Edward Downes built Oniton Hall because he wanted
options: A a place for discussing politics.
B a place to display his wealth.
C a place for artists and writers.

questions.content: Visitors can learn about the work of servants in the past from
What is new for children at Oniton Hall?

sections.instruction: Which activity is offered at each of the following locations on the farm? Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-H, next to Questions 15-20.

questions.content: Match each farm location with the activity offered there.
options: F seeing rare breeds of animals
G helping to look after animals

sections.passage_or_context: Which TWO things do the students agree they need to include in their reviews of Romeo and Juliet?

questions.content: Which TWO things do the students agree they need to include in their reviews of Romeo and Juliet?
options: B a summary of the plot
C a description of the theatre
E a reference to particular scenes

sections.instruction: Which opinion do the speakers give about each of the following aspects of The Emporium’s production of Romeo and Juliet? Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 23-27.

questions.content: Match each aspect of the production with the opinion given by the speakers.
options: A They both expected this to be more traditional.
B They both thought this was original.
C They agree this created the right atmosphere.
D They agree this was a major strength.
E They were both disappointed by this.
F They disagree about why this was an issue.
G They disagree about how this could be improved.

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.content: The students think the story of Romeo and Juliet is still relevant for young people today because
options: A it illustrates how easily conflict can start.
B it deals with problems that families experience.
C it teaches them about relationships.

questions.content: The students found watching Romeo and Juliet in another language
Why do the students think Shakespeare’s plays have such international appeal?
options: C They can be interpreted in many ways.

sections.passage_or_context:
The impact of digital technology on the Icelandic language

The Icelandic language
• has approximately (31) {{BLANK}} speakers
• has a (32) {{BLANK}} that is still growing
• has not changed a lot over the last thousand years
• has its own words for computer-based concepts, such as web browser and (33) {{BLANK}}

Young speakers
• are big users of digital technology, such as (34) {{BLANK}}
• are becoming (35) {{BLANK}} very quickly
• are having discussions using only English while they are in the (36) {{BLANK}} at school
• are better able to identify the content of a (37) {{BLANK}} in English than Icelandic

Technology and internet companies
• write very little in Icelandic because of the small number of speakers and because of how complicated its (38) {{BLANK}} is

The Icelandic government
• has set up a fund to support the production of more digital content in the language
• believes that Icelandic has a secure future
• is worried that young Icelanders may lose their (39) {{BLANK}} as Icelanders
• is worried about the consequences of children not being (40) {{BLANK}} in either Icelandic or English
